assembled_draft_consistency_log_findings: CC-03: Implementation section B.3.2 uses abbreviated partner names 'EuroLog International' and 'Boreal AI Labs' omitting legal suffixes 'AG' and 'Oy' per partners.json — flagged as inconsistency_flagged

CC-04: D4-01 described as 'coordination protocol specification (M18)' in excellence section and as 'formal standardisation proposal... by M48' in impact section — flagged as inconsistency_flagged requiring resolution

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO addresses all four mandatory call scope areas (SR-02 robust planning, SR-03 memory management, SR-04 external tool integration, SR-05 multi-agent coordination) in a unified integrated architecture
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json SR-02 (mandatory: true), SR-03 (mandatory: true), SR-04 (mandatory: true), SR-05 (mandatory: true); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/concept_note.md Pillars 1–3 and Cross-Cutting Capability sections

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-1 target: ≥40% improvement in plan success rate over LLM-based baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on standardised benchmarks (WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA) for tasks requiring more than 10 sequential steps
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json OBJ-1.measurable_target: 'Achieve ≥40% improvement in plan success rate over pure LLM-based agent baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on standardised agent benchmarks (WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA) for tasks requiring >10 sequential steps'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-2 target: ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in interaction sequences exceeding 50 agent steps
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json OBJ-2.measurable_target: 'Demonstrate ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in interaction sequences exceeding 50 agent steps'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-3 target: effective coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents, ≥25% joint task completion improvement over independent baselines, formal mathematical proof of protocol convergence
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json OBJ-3.measurable_target: 'Demonstrate effective coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents on shared tasks, with ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent agent baselines, and formal proof of protocol convergence'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-8 target: ≥30 external tools/APIs integrated; tool invocation success rate ≥95%; mean recovery time from tool failure ≤5s; full provenance traces for 100% of invocations
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json OBJ-8.measurable_target: 'Typed tool registry supporting ≥30 external tools/APIs across all three demonstrator domains; tool invocation success rate ≥95% with verified output validation; mean recovery time from tool failure ≤5s; full provenance traces for 100% of tool invocations'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-4 target: diagnostic accuracy within 10% of specialist clinicians on 500 de-identified cases; OBJ-5 target: ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy reduction at ISIA pilot factory; OBJ-6 target: ≥20% disruption recovery improvement and ≥15% delivery adherence improvement across 3 corridors

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Current AI agent research operates in three largely disconnected communities (LLM-based agents, classical planning/MAS, memory-augmented neural systems); MAESTRO's contribution is co-design across all three
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/strategic_positioning.md section 'Positioning Against the State of the Art': 'Current AI agent research is concentrated in three largely disconnected communities: LLM-based agents… Classical planning and multi-agent systems… Memory-augmented neural systems… MAESTRO's distinctive contribution is the co-design of these three capabilities'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP2 tasks T2-01 (sub-task graph decomposition with formal pre/post-conditions), T2-02 (MCTS and HTN planning integration with LLM heuristics), T2-03 (plan monitoring and self-correction) implement neuro-symbolic planning
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json WP2.tasks: T2-01.title='Sub-task graph decomposition design with formal pre/post-conditions'; T2-02.title='MCTS and HTN planning integration with LLM heuristics'; T2-03.title='Plan monitoring and self-correction implementation'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP3 implements tri-store memory architecture (working, episodic, semantic) with learned controller via tasks T3-01–T3-03; WP4 develops formally specified coordination protocols with convergence proofs via tasks T4-01–T4-02

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: IPR open-core strategy: core framework, benchmark, evaluation infrastructure, and coordination protocol specification under Apache 2.0; sector-specific demonstrator configurations protectable as trade secrets by NIHS, ISIA, ELI; BAL leads IP management (WP9-T9-02)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase5_impact_architecture/impact_architecture.json exploitation_plan.ipr_strategy: 'open-core IPR strategy... core MAESTRO framework, benchmark suite, evaluation infrastructure, and coordination protocol specifications are released under the Apache 2.0 open-source licence... Sector-specific demonstrator software configurations... may be protected as trade secrets... by NIHS for the clinical decision support configuration, ISIA for the manufacturing optimisation deployment, and ELI for the logistics integration. BAL leads IPR management coordination under WP9'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: CERIA submits formal standardisation proposal identified as D4-01 to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48 (KPI-08); D4-01 characterized as the standardisation submission due M48
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/impacts.json IMP-6.linked_deliverable_ids=['D4-01']; docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase5_impact_architecture/impact_architecture.json KPI-08.traceable_to_deliverable='D4-01', target='≥1 formal submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE Standards Association by M48'. CONTRADICTED BY: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json WP4.deliverables D4-01.title='Multi-agent coordination protocol specification', type='report', due_month=18
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: D4-01 is characterized in the impact section (B.2.1, B.2.2) as the 'formal standardisation proposal' submitted to ISO/IEC by M48, but the authoritative structural source (wp_structure.json) defines D4-01 as 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification', type='report', due_month=18. The M48 submission target and 'formal standardisation proposal' description in the impact section are inconsistent with the M18 protocol specification report characterization in wp_structure.json. This inconsistency is also flagged in assembled draft consistency_log CC-04 as requiring resolution. Status is Unresolved pending correction of either the deliverable description in the impact section or the WP structure definition of D4-01.

Conduct integration checkpoints every 6 months'; RISK-01.mitigation: 'Adopt a modular architecture with well-defined interfaces... Implement fallback modes where the LLM-only planner operates'; RISK-07.mitigation: 'Design all MAESTRO components with model-agnostic interfaces'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three governance bodies: General Assembly (all 8 partners, biannual, strategic authority), Project Management Committee (all 8 partners, quarterly, operational management), Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU and NIHS, minimum biannual)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Partner legal names for ELI and BAL as stated in implementation section B.3.2 partner descriptions
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json ELI.legal_name='EuroLog International AG'; BAL.legal_name='Boreal AI Labs Oy'. Implementation section B.3.2 text uses 'EuroLog International' (omitting 'AG') and 'Boreal AI Labs' (omitting 'Oy').
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Implementation section B.3.2 uses abbreviated partner names 'EuroLog International' and 'Boreal AI Labs' that omit the legal entity suffixes 'AG' and 'Oy' respectively. The authoritative source docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json records legal names as 'EuroLog International AG' (partner 6) and 'Boreal AI Labs Oy' (partner 7). This name inconsistency is also flagged in the assembled draft consistency_log CC-03 as 'inconsistency_flagged' with 'Correction required in Implementation section B.3.2 partner description text to match canonical legal names.' Unresolved pending correction.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: D4-01 is described as 'coordination protocol specification (D4-01, M18)' in excellence section B.1.2 but as 'formal standardisation proposal (D4-01)... by M48' in impact section B.2.1 and B.2.2 — materially different characterizations and incompatible due months for the same deliverable ID
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json WP4.deliverables D4-01.title='Multi-agent coordination protocol specification', type='report', due_month=18 — supports excellence section characterization. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/impacts.json IMP-6.linked_deliverable_ids=['D4-01']; docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase5_impact_architecture/impact_architecture.json KPI-08.traceable_to_deliverable='D4-01', target month M48 — supports impact section characterization. Sources are in mutual contradiction for the role and due month of D4-01.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Excellence section B.1.2 characterizes D4-01 as 'coordination protocol specification (D4-01, M18)' per wp_structure.json (due_month=18, type='report'). Impact section B.2.1 and B.2.2 characterize D4-01 as the 'formal standardisation proposal' submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE 'by M48'. These are materially different descriptions — different type (protocol spec vs. standardisation proposal) and incompatible due months (M18 vs. M48) for the same deliverable ID. This cross-section inconsistency is confirmed by assembled draft consistency_log CC-04 as 'inconsistency_flagged' with 'Resolution required: either D4-01 is the M18 protocol specification (and a separate deliverable ID should cover the M48 standardisation submission), or the Excellence section description and month must be corrected.' Unresolved pending resolution of CC-04.

resolution_required.issue: D4-01 described as standardisation proposal (M48) in impact section; wp_structure.json defines it as protocol specification report due M18. Clarification required on whether D4-01 is due M18 (as per wp_structure.json) or represents the M48 standardisation submission, and whether a separate deliverable ID should cover the standardisation submission activity.

Implementation section B.3.2 uses abbreviated legal names 'EuroLog International' and 'Boreal AI Labs'; authoritative partners.json has 'EuroLog International AG' and 'Boreal AI Labs Oy'. Correction required.

Material cross-section inconsistency in D4-01 characterization between excellence (M18 protocol spec) and impact (M48 standardisation proposal) sections. Requires resolution before proposal finalization. See also assembled draft consistency_log CC-04.
